signals.reasoning: The event veto is clear, but BTC is still range-bound and the macro regime remains weak at RED 42. There is not enough trend clarity here to justify a fresh position.
ETH is still trading like a weak major in post-flush stabilization. With macro still fragile, I would rather stay neutral here than force a view.
SOL still has some squeeze fuel from prior long liquidations, but the bounce lacks enough strength to justify a fresh long under a weak macro backdrop. It stays watch-only.
DASH is weak on the day, but the flush is already mature and the current tape is not offering a clean continuation short. It is no longer a high-quality fresh entry.
TST has already squeezed hard and remains too thin for a confident new chase. The easy part of the move looks gone.
AAVE has a decent rebound, but it has already rolled off the highs and does not have the same clean squeeze pressure as the better alt candidates. Neutral is the better call.

ZEC is one of the cleaner bearish names on the board because price is still soft and open interest continues to build on the short side. Confidence stays reduced because the macro backdrop is weak but not fully risk-off and the move is not a collapse yet.
STBL still has crowded-long characteristics, but it is holding up well enough that the short is not clean here. It remains a watchlist fade rather than an active one.
GOAT is still too damaged and too unstable to trust on either side. It is not a clean new short and it has not rebuilt enough structure to be a serious long.

JTO remains one of the cleaner squeeze-long setups, with strong negative funding and better liquidity than most of the other long candidates. Confidence is capped because macro is still weak and the move is no longer early, but it still earns a reduced-size long upgrade.

0G still has a valid squeeze-long profile with deeply negative funding and supportive price/OI behavior, but it is much more extended and volatile than JTO. That keeps it as a lower-confidence secondary long rather than the top pick.
